metadata.title: Core SPC for Human Plasma Derived Coagulation Factor VII Products

rules.rule_text: Factor VII potency must be determined using the European Pharmacopoeia chromogenic assay. The reconstituted product must declare the content of coagulation factors II, IX, and X.
rules.verbatim_quote: The potency (IU) is determined using the European Pharmacopoeia chromogenic assay. ... The reconstituted product contains less than {alpha}, {beta} and {gamma} IU/ml of coagulation factors II, IX and X, respectively.

rules.rule_text: Factor VII products are indicated for treatment and prophylaxis of bleeding disorders caused by isolated congenital factor VII deficiency. Prophylaxis is indicated only when associated with a history of bleeding and residual FVII:C below 25% of normal (0.25 IU/ml).
rules.verbatim_quote: Treatment of bleeding disorders caused by isolated congenital factor VII deficiency. Prophylaxis of bleeding disorders caused by isolated congenital factor VII deficiency associated with a history of bleeding and a residual level of factor VII:C lower than 25% of normal (0.25 IU/ml).

rules.rule_text: Factor VII products do not contain activated factor VIIa and must not be used in haemophiliac patients with inhibitor.
rules.verbatim_quote: This preparation does not contain activated factor VIIa and should not be used in haemophiliac patients with inhibitor.

rules.rule_text: Dosage intervals must be adapted to the short circulating half-life of factor VII (approximately 3-5 hours). When administered by intermittent injections/infusions, intervals of 6-8 hours are often adequate.
rules.verbatim_quote: Dosage intervals must be adapted to the short circulating half-life of factor VII of approximately 3 to 5 hours. When {(invented) name of product} is administrated by intermittent injections/infusions, dosage intervals of 6-8 hours are often adequate.

rules.rule_text: For major surgical interventions, FVII level must be >0.50 IU/ml pre-operatively, then maintained at 0.25-0.45 IU/ml (trough-peak) for 8-10 days or until complete wound healing.
rules.verbatim_quote: Major surgical interventions Pre-operative > 0.50 Then 0.25-0.45 (trough-peak) For 8 to 10 days or until complete wound healing

rules.rule_text: Factor VII products are contraindicated in patients at high risk of thrombosis or disseminated intravascular coagulation. If heparin is a constituent, known heparin allergy or history of HIT is also a contraindication.
rules.verbatim_quote: High risk of thrombosis or disseminated intravascular coagulation (see 4.4). ... Known allergy to heparin or history of heparin induced thrombocytopenia
rules.exceptions: Heparin contraindication only applies when heparin is a constituent.

rules.rule_text: Patients given FVII products must be observed closely for signs or symptoms of intravascular coagulation or thrombosis. Caution must be exercised in patients with coronary heart disease, liver disease, post-operative patients, neonates, and patients at risk of thromboembolic phenomena or DIC.
rules.verbatim_quote: There is a risk of thrombosis or disseminated intravascular coagulation when patients are treated with human coagulation factor VII containing product. Patients given human coagulation factor VII should be observed closely for signs or symptoms of intravascular coagulation or thrombosis. Because of the risk of thromboembolic complications, caution should be exercised when administering human coagulation factor VII to patients with a history of coronary heart disease, to patients with liver disease, to patients post-operatively, to neonates, or to patients at risk of thromboembolic phenomena or disseminated intravascular coagulation.

rules.rule_text: The SmPC must warn that overdose with FVII products (prothrombin complex products) has been associated with myocardial infarction, DIC, venous thrombosis, and pulmonary embolism.
rules.verbatim_quote: The use of high doses of factor VII containing products (prothrombin complex products) has been associated with instances of myocardial infarction, disseminated intravascular coagulation, venous thrombosis and pulmonary embolism.

rules.rule_text: When performing clotting tests sensitive to heparin in patients receiving high doses of FVII, the heparin administered with the product must be taken into account.
rules.verbatim_quote: When performing clotting tests which are sensitive to heparin in patients receiving high doses of human factor VII, the heparin administered with the product must be taken into account.
rules.exceptions: Only applicable when product contains heparin.

rules.rule_text: The SmPC PK section must include data on incremental recovery, AUC, half-life (initial phase and elimination), mean residence time (MRT), and clearance.
rules.verbatim_quote: Description of incremental recovery, area under the curve (AUC), half-life (both the initial phase and elimination half life), mean residence time (MRT), clearance

rules.rule_text: Data on children less than 6 years of age treated with the product must be described in the SmPC.
rules.verbatim_quote: Data on children less than 6 years of age treated with the product should be described.

rules.rule_text: The product must not be mixed with other medicinal products including heparin. Only provided injection/infusion sets should be used due to risk of FVII adsorption.
rules.verbatim_quote: This medicinal product must not be mixed with other medicinal products including heparin. ... Only the provided <injection> <infusion> sets should be used because treatment failure can occur as a consequence of human plasma factor VII adsorption to the internal surface of some <injection><infusion> equipment.
